description_long: This is a vintage wooden nightstand with a square tabletop featuring a prominent star-shaped inlay at its center, bordered by a lighter wood trim. The nightstand has a single drawer with a round, ornate metal knob, and the drawer front is framed by a beveled edge. The legs are turned and carved, connected by decorative stretchers that add both stability and visual interest. The wood has a rich, medium-brown finish, and the craftsmanship suggests a traditional or antique style. The overall construction appears sturdy, with visible wood grain and some subtle wear that adds character. This piece would typically be found in a residential or urban setting, possibly repurposed for outdoor or sidewalk use.
description: A vintage wooden nightstand with a star inlay on top, a single drawer with a round knob, and turned legs connected by decorative stretchers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the star inlay, single drawer with a round knob, and detailed leg carvings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the thickness of the tabletop, the side panel, and the turned leg profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back panel and the connecting stretchers between the legs.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the side panel and leg details without the drawer or knob visible.
attribute_leg_design: turned wooden legs with decorative stretchers
attribute_placement_relative_to_other_ur: beside a bed or against a wall
